perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Non -Public Information shall not include: i. Information that is or becomes available in the public domain through no fault or action of Data Recipient ; ii. Information that was or is acquired by Data Recipient from a source other than WECC in a manner which is not otherwise subject to confi dentiality restrictions; or iii. Information that was or is independently developed by Data Recipient as demonstrated by Data Recipient’s documentation .
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Non -Public Information shall not include: i. Information that is or becomes available in the public domain if released by a third party without WECC's consent; ii. Information that was or is acquired by Data Recipient from a source other than WECC in a manner which is not otherwise subject to confi dentiality restrictions; or iii. Information that was or is independently developed by Data Recipient as demonstrated by Data Recipient’s documentation .
perturbation.explanation: By modifying Section 1.b.i to state 'Information that is or becomes available in the public domain if released by a third party without WECC's consent', we contradict the original intent of excluding publicly available information from the definition of Non-Public Information. This perturbation implies that information already in the public domain becomes Non-Public if released by a third party without WECC’s consent, which is nonsensical and causes in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Non -Public Information shall not include: i. Information that is or becomes available in the public domain if released by a third party without WECC's consent; ii. Information that was or is acquired by Data Recipient from a source other than WECC in a manner which is not otherwise subject to confi dentiality restrictions; or iii. Information that was or is independently developed by Data Recipient as demonstrated by Data Recipient’s documentation .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Non-Public Information' to include information that is publicly available if released by a third party without WECC's consent. This directly contradicts the original intention of excluding information already in the public domain from the definition of Non-Public Information, creating a logical inconsistency and in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation, and falling under the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated by WECC or Data Recipient at any time at their sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated by WECC at any time at its sole discretion. Data Recipient may only terminate this agreement if WECC is in breach of its obligations and fails to cure such breach within 90 days of written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying Section 7.a to restrict Data Recipient's ability to terminate the agreement creates an imbalance. The original clause allowed either party to terminate at any time, while the change requires Data Recipient to prove WECC's breach and wait 90 days. This now contradicts with the section before (7.a), where it mentioned that This Agreement may be terminated by WECC or Data Recipient at any time at their sole discretion. This introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated by WECC at any time at its sole discretion. Data Recipient may only terminate this agreement if WECC is in breach of its obligations and fails to cure such breach within 90 days of written notice.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that both WECC and Data Recipient could terminate the agreement at any time at their sole discretion. The changed text modifies this, restricting Data Recipient's ability to terminate the agreement to only when WECC is in breach and fails to cure within 90 days. This directly contradicts the original agreement where the Data Recipient could terminate the agreement at any time. The contradicted text is in the same section (7.a), where it mentioned that This Agreement may be terminated by WECC or Data Recipient at any time at their sole discretion. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed , except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination .
perturbation.changed_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement initiated by WECC, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed , except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination . However, if Data recipient initiates the termination of the agreement, Data Recipient can keep a copy of Non-Public information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding that Data Recipient can keep a copy of Non-Public information, if Data recipient initiates the termination of the agreement, this contradicts the general idea of the confidentiality agreement, and now creates uncertainty regarding what happens when the agreement ends. The in-text contradiction is about giving different options of when the agreement is over, depending on who started the termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon any termination of this Agreement initiated by WECC, Data Recipient shall return to WECC all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession or destroy all Non -Public Information in Data Recipient’s possession and certify to WECC in writing that all Non -Public Information has been returned or destroyed , except as may be otherwise required by law in which case the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement shall survive termination . However, if Data recipient initiates the termination of the agreement, Data Recipient can keep a copy of Non-Public information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text mandates the return or destruction of all Non-Public Information upon termination of the agreement. The changed text creates an exception, allowing the Data Recipient to keep a copy of the Non-Public Information if the Data Recipient initiates the termination. This directly contradicts the previous requirement to return or destroy all Non-Public Information, creating an in-text contradiction as per the explanation provided.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can only be modified by written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can be modified by either a written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient or through a notification via email from WECC to Data Recipient, which is considered accepted unless Data Recipient objects within 48 hours.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'or through a notification via email from WECC to Data Recipient, which is considered accepted unless Data Recipient objects within 48 hours' to Section 8.b, we introduce a contradiction about how the agreement can be modified. The original text states that modifications can only be done through a written agreement executed by both parties, but the addition allows WECC to unilaterally modify the agreement via email notification unless the Data Recipient objects in 48 hours. This new clause makes it uncertain how the agreement can be modified creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding between WECC and Data Recipient related to the Non -Public Information and supersedes all previous communications between WECC and Data Recipient related to this subject. This Agreement can be modified by either a written agreement executed by WECC and Data Recipient or through a notification via email from WECC to Data Recipient, which is considered accepted unless Data Recipient objects within 48 hours.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement modification process, stating it can be modified by written agreement or email notification with a 48-hour objection window. This contradicts the initial statement in both the original text and the contradicted text (which are identical) that the agreement can *only* be modified by a written agreement executed by both parties. This introduces a direct conflict, fitting the definition of a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction. The explanation provided clearly states how this change leads to uncertainty and contradiction.
